vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The biome is in a state of critical failure. The expected specimens (P1-P4) are not visible as living botanical entities. The pots contain only soil and debris.

plant_audit.P1_Yellow_Pot: Empty/Soil only. No String of Nickels present.
plant_audit.P2_Black_Pot: Empty/Soil only. No Mexican Mint present.
plant_audit.P3_Black_Pot: Empty/Soil only. No Pothos or White Rabbit anchor present.
plant_audit.P4_Shared_Black_Pot: Empty/Soil only. No Silver Guest present.

biome_observations.soil_condition: Appears desiccated and dark; no signs of active root systems or moisture.
biome_observations.debris: Presence of white, fragmented material (likely eggshell or mineral deposits) on the surface of the black pot.
biome_observations.desk_surface: Clean, no organic matter or biological growth detected.

vision_report.temporal_deltas: Over the 5-day sequence, there is zero evidence of plant growth, recovery, or movement. The state is static and necrotic.
vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is non-viable. All registered plants have suffered total mortality or have been removed from the pots.
vision_report.anomalies: The 'White Rabbit' anchor is missing from P3. The pots are effectively abandoned.
vision_report.narrative_description: As a botanical observer, I have performed a meticulous audit of the provided image sequence. The registry provided (P1-P4) does not match the physical reality of the pots, which are devoid of any living plant material. The sequence shows a consistent, unchanging state of empty soil. The 'White Rabbit' anchor, which should be present in P3, is absent. I conclude that the biome has suffered a total systemic loss of all specimens.
